assumptions: Traffic assumed ~<50k requests/month (no intake answer); right-sized accordingly — scale up instances for higher sustained throughput
Downtime tolerance assumed low (no intake answer); budget = single-AZ, balanced = multi-AZ
No compliance regime specified; standard security floor applied
CPU-bound ML inference rules out Lambda (execution time, CPU ceiling, model artifact size); always-on container shape chosen for all tiers
Model artifacts stored in S3 and loaded at task startup; no GPU assumed (CPU inference)
Sustained throughput means provisioned RDS not Aurora Serverless; ElastiCache for inference result caching
Inference results are cacheable (same input → same output); ElastiCache write-through cache assumed
Budget tier collapses to single EC2 box (Docker Compose) — no ALB, no NAT, no Fargate, no RDS managed split
Alerting email destination assumed; Slack/PagerDuty added at balanced/resilient tiers
Cost estimates are on-demand list prices for us-east-1, presented as ranges over assumed monthly volumes. They exclude the AWS Free Tier, Savings Plans, Reserved Instances, and negotiated discounts.
Assumed traffic ~100k–1M requests/month, applied equally to all three tiers (tiers differ by robustness, not traffic). Set "expected monthly visitors" in intake to change it.
clarificationsUsed: Reference architecture: Container API — steady/high sustained traffic, CPU-bound, ALB+Fargate+RDS+ElastiCache+CloudFront+WAF at scale
Budget tier collapsed to single-box per cost hierarchy rules (serverless does not fit CPU-bound sustained workload; managed quartet deferred to balanced delta)

tiers.name: budget
tiers.summary: Single EC2 (c6g.large) in a public subnet running Docker Compose: inference API container + self-managed Postgres + Redis. Model artifacts on EBS gp3. CloudFront+WAF at edge. No ALB, no NAT, no Fargate, no managed RDS — ~$0.09/hr compute floor.
delta: Single EC2 c6g.large (public subnet, EBS gp3) — no ALB, no NAT gateway, no Fargate, no managed RDS
Docker Compose: inference API + self-managed Postgres + Redis co-located on one host
Model artifacts on EBS gp3 (loaded at startup); backups via nightly pg_dump → S3
CloudFront+WAF at edge; restrictive SG allows 443 from CloudFront IP ranges only
CloudWatch Logs + golden-signal alarms → SNS email for operator alerting
Single-AZ; no horizontal scale; host failure = manual recovery (balanced adds HA)
tradeoffs: Single AZ: host failure requires snapshot restore — no automated failover
Self-managed Postgres/Redis: operator owns patching, vacuuming, memory tuning
No ALB/Fargate: zero horizontal scale within this tier — scale up instance type only
Cheapest always-on floor (~$0.09/hr c6g.large + EBS); balanced adds ~$150+/mo for HA managed stack

costDrivers.note: Data transfer out to internet, first 10TB/month tier (North America). First 1TB/month is free.
HTTPS requests ($0.0075 per 10,000). North America/Europe.
Per web ACL/month. Add ~$1.00 per rule/month and ~$0.60 per 1M requests inspected.
Assumed-throughput estimate: t4g.small on-demand (us-east-1). Cost = instance count × running hours; storage/IO billed separately, multi-AZ via the tier multiplier.
gp3 general-purpose SSD volume, $0.08/GB-month. The durable home for an embedded file DB (SQLite/DuckDB) pinned to one compute node — never EFS/NFS (file locking is unreliable over a network FS).
S3 Standard storage, first 50TB/month tier.
PUT/COPY/POST/LIST requests per 1,000. GET/SELECT requests are ~$0.0004 per 1,000.
CloudWatch Logs ingestion $0.50/GB (first 10GB/mo free in some accounts). Archival storage is cheaper; Insights queries extra.
Standard topic API requests ($0.50 per 1M). Delivery (HTTP/SMS/email) priced separately.

tiers.name: balanced
tiers.summary: Multi-AZ Fargate + managed RDS Postgres + ElastiCache Redis in private subnets behind ALB. Model artifacts from S3 at task startup. CloudFront+WAF retained. Adds X-Ray tracing, CloudWatch dashboards, Slack alerting.

edges.payload: model artifact pull at task startup

delta: Single EC2 box → Fargate ECS service in private subnets (multi-AZ, auto-scaling on CPU target 60%)
Self-managed Postgres → RDS Postgres db.t4g.large (multi-AZ standby, automated backups)
Self-managed Redis → ElastiCache cache.r7g.large (Multi-AZ with replica, in-transit TLS)
Added ALB (multi-AZ) as Fargate front-door; CloudFront → ALB origin
Added NAT Gateway (per AZ) for private-subnet egress to S3/ECR/Secrets Manager
Added X-Ray distributed tracing + CloudWatch Dashboards
Slack alerting added alongside email SNS for critical alarms
Fargate auto-scaling absorbs burst; CloudFront caching offloads repeated identical inference inputs
tradeoffs: NAT Gateway adds always-on ~$33/mo per AZ egress cost (private-subnet requirement for Fargate+RDS)
ALB adds always-on LCU cost; justified by multi-AZ Fargate target group
RDS Multi-AZ standby doubles RDS cost vs single-AZ; buys automated failover ~60s RTO
No cross-region DR — a regional outage requires manual intervention (resilient tier adds that)

Standard topic API requests ($0.50 per 1M). Delivery (HTTP/SMS/email) priced separately.
Application Load Balancer per-hour charge (always-on).
Assumed-throughput estimate: per Load Balancer Capacity Unit-hour; LCUs scale with connections, requests, and bandwidth.
Assumed-throughput estimate: per GB memory-hour (Linux/x86). Total = memoryGB x hours.
Assumed-throughput estimate: per vCPU-hour (Linux/x86). Total = vCPU x hours.
Assumed-throughput estimate: db.t4g.large on-demand (us-east-1). Cost = instance count × running hours; storage/IO billed separately, multi-AZ via the tier multiplier.
Assumed-throughput estimate: cache.t4g.medium on-demand (us-east-1). Cost = instance count × running hours; storage/IO billed separately, multi-AZ via the tier multiplier.
Per GB processed. Forced by the private-subnet data-tier default (security baseline no-public-data-tier); the most common budget surprise.
Per NAT gateway-hour (always-on, ~$32.40/month per AZ before data processing).
X-Ray traces: $5.00 per 1M traces recorded (first 100k/mo free).
Compute duration: $0.0000166667 per GB-second (x86). Multiply by memoryGB x durationSeconds per invocation.
On-demand list price ($0.20 per 1M requests). Excludes Free Tier.
Required by the private-subnet security default (no public data tier) — the most common budget surprise. Inter-AZ data transfer, charged $0.01/GB in each direction.
Required by the private-subnet security default (no public data tier) — the most common budget surprise. Data transfer out to internet, first 10TB/month tier. First 100GB/month is free.

tiers.name: resilient
tiers.summary: Adds RDS cross-region read replica + promoted standby for DR, DynamoDB Global Tables for idempotency store, Route 53 health-check failover, EventBridge for async inference fan-out, FIFO SQS queue with DLQ, PagerDuty escalation, and CloudWatch anomaly detection.

edges.payload: model artifact pull at task startup

delta: Route 53 health-check failover added for DNS-level regional DR routing
RDS cross-region read replica added (promotable in DR event, ~60 min RPO without binlog shipping)
ElastiCache Global Datastore replicates cache to DR region
FIFO SQS queue + async Fargate inference worker added for burst-buffering and ordered async jobs
DynamoDB Global Tables for cross-region idempotency key store (exactly-once processing guarantee)
EventBridge bus for inference-complete fan-out (decouples result routing from synchronous path)
CloudWatch Anomaly Detection adds ML-baseline alarms on latency + error rate
PagerDuty escalation Lambda replaces/augments Slack for P1 incidents
tradeoffs: Cross-region replication (RDS replica, Global Datastore, Global Tables) adds significant data-transfer + resource cost
FIFO SQS + async worker adds complexity; accept higher p99 latency for async path vs synchronous
Global Tables on-demand: cost scales with write throughput; monitor idempotency key TTL aggressively
DR failover still requires operator decision to promote RDS replica; fully automated requires Aurora Global + Route 53 arc

keyDecisions.chosen: Fargate (ECS) at balanced/resilient; single EC2 (Docker Compose) at budget
alternativesConsidered: Lambda (ruled out: CPU ceiling, cold-start latency, 15-min timeout, model artifact size)
EC2 ASG (more ops overhead than Fargate; Fargate preferred for balanced+)
EKS (over-engineered for this scope; Fargate/ECS sufficient)
keyDecisions.rationale: Fargate gives horizontal, AZ-aware scaling with zero node management — the right Performance Efficiency + Operational Excellence trade-off for sustained CPU-bound workloads; budget collapses to one EC2 box to avoid the always-on Fargate+ALB+NAT floor.

alternativesConsidered: In-process LRU cache (lost on task restart, not shared across tasks)
DynamoDB item cache (higher latency, not ideal for hot-path sub-ms reads)
No cache (every request hits full inference stack — CPU waste at sustained throughput)
keyDecisions.rationale: A shared Redis cache absorbs repeated identical inputs, protecting CPU capacity and the database from hot-read bursts — the highest-value Performance Efficiency + Cost Optimization lever for ML inference with recurring queries.

keyDecisions.decision: Primary datastore for request metadata / results
keyDecisions.chosen: RDS Postgres (db.t4g.medium budget via self-managed; db.t4g.large managed at balanced)
alternativesConsidered: DynamoDB (poor fit for relational metadata, ad-hoc query patterns on inference logs)
Aurora Serverless v2 (good fit for spiky, not sustained; provisioned RDS cheaper at steady throughput)
keyDecisions.rationale: Provisioned RDS Postgres fits sustained, predictable throughput — better cost efficiency than Aurora Serverless v2 under constant load (Cost Optimization pillar).

keyDecisions.decision: Single-box budget shape vs. managed quartet
keyDecisions.chosen: Single EC2 (c6g.large) in public subnet, Docker Compose: API container + self-managed Postgres + Redis, model artifacts on EBS gp3
alternativesConsidered: Fargate + RDS + ElastiCache + ALB + NAT at budget (always-on ~$150+/mo floor before traffic)
Lambda for inference (CPU-bound, model size, latency constraints rule it out)
keyDecisions.rationale: Co-locating API, Postgres, and Redis on one c6g.large eliminates the ALB+NAT+Fargate+RDS always-on floor — the Cost Optimization baseline that the balanced delta then splits into managed, HA services.

keyDecisions.chosen: S3 (model artifacts) loaded into container memory at ECS task startup; EBS gp3 for single-box budget
alternativesConsidered: EFS (not suitable for large binary model reads at startup; higher latency than S3 presigned pull)
Baking model into container image (bloats ECR image; slows deploys; couples model version to code version)
keyDecisions.rationale: S3 decouples model versioning from code, enables blue/green model swaps without image rebuilds, and costs near-zero at rest — Operational Excellence + Cost Optimization.

securityFloor: Encrypt all data at rest (KMS / service-native SSE), preferring customer-managed keys.
Enforce TLS in transit everywhere; terminate HTTPS at the edge and reject plaintext.
Least-privilege IAM: scoped per-service roles, no wildcards, no long-lived access keys.
Enable S3 Block Public Access account-wide so no bucket or object can be made public.
Keep the data tier private: VPC-bound stores in private subnets with no public route; managed/serverless stores (DynamoDB/S3) are already private via IAM and need no VPC or NAT.
Store secrets in AWS Secrets Manager / SSM Parameter Store — never in code or images.
Front public endpoints with CloudFront + AWS WAF (managed + rate-based rules); Shield Standard for L3/L4.
Enable CloudTrail plus S3/CloudFront/ALB access logs and VPC Flow Logs for audit.
